title: storage-driver btrfs not supported
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**BUG REPORT**

/kind bug

Podman is refusing to work with btrfs as storage-driver

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Easy way:
Specify desired storage driver as parameter
`podman --storage-driver btrfs info`

"Right" way:
1. adjust storage.conf

Change
```
[storage]
  driver = "vfs"
```
to
```
[storage]
  driver = "btrfs"
```

2. Empty $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/

Make sure $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/
is emtpy or emtpy it

3. run podman info or any other podman comand
`podman info`

**Describe the results you received:**
ERRO[0000] Failed to GetDriver graph btrfs /home/koko/.local/share/containers/storage 
Error: could not get runtime: failed to GetDriver graph btrfs /home/koko/.local/share/containers/storage: driver not supported

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman info page, something similiar to the **vfs** version:

host:

  CgroupVersion: v1

Release: 18.04
Codename: bionic

comments.body: This is an issue with the way the package was built, the BUILD TAGS are disabling btrfs support.
Please open a bugzilla with the distro that you got the package from.

@rhatdan the package is from the open suse build server as described in the installation docs. The problem remains with podman 1.8.0-2 .. distro is Ubuntu lts 18.04 as described in the initial issue text
Reopening, we do handle OBS issues here for lack of a better venue
@delight should be fixed by `1.8.0~5`, will be available in an hour or less. Please re-open if it doesn't.

tltr;
In general the btrfs storage driver is loading but it is unusable because of the problem with *not* removing the created subvolumes, resulting in all sort of false behaviour

@lsm5 just tryed 1.8.0~5 and 1.8.0~6 

Both are able to start the btrfs storage drive.
Downloading the image and starting a container works. Podman creates subvolumes in btrfs as expected. 
Removing thou won't work correctly.

Simplest way of reproducing the error is something like 
`podman pull ubuntu
`
and then trying to delete it again
`podman rmi ubuntu`

result in
Error: remove /home/koko/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs/subvolumes/27b97a061bd85ff57a9e5a6d40205f5c600d09319d23baaf69a4f0bd468c0ae7: operation not permitted

This behavior results ultimately in a bunch of unexpected errors e.g.:
1) When a container is created and deleted again
ERRO[0024] Error removing container a8819fc16eef827bca67f8642dda849f78000c07d1b4fe893c8f1926afd53c3f: error removing container a8819fc16eef827bca67f8642dda849f78000c07d1b4fe893c8f1926afd53c3f root filesystem: remove /home/koko/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs/subvolumes/53f23057d3f31b4992ff75faa784cbb3f8d3ef275b61eab04ecccc216bb0df94: operation not permitted 

and even though `podman ps -a` is an empty list `podman rmi ubuntu` will result in an error like:
A container associated with containers/storage, i.e. via Buildah, CRI-O, etc., may be associated with this image: ccc6e87d482b
Error: image is in use by a container

2) When trying to remove a just downloaded image the initially described permission error occurs ... now the image is not in the list anymore `podman image ls` but when trying to download the image again it acts like the image layers are already there ... resulting in wrong image.

Only workaround I found is to remove the btrfs subvolumes after removing something with podman ... though this is not really a workaround because you won't be able to tell easily which subvolume hash is associated with a certain image or container.

I guess the problem is already known see #3963 

something around https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/1c8d809f3db40ab852112332457e2ea7a09ba859/drivers/btrfs/btrfs.go#L635 ? 

I'll try the mount option to see if it works ;-) 
If it works and you'll let me know, where we could put the info on the documentation pages I can create a little pull request on that topic.

I'll get in touch once I tried.

OK, using the mount option "user_subvol_rm_alowed" is a pragmatic workaround IMO .. a little documentation on the topic would be desirable. I'll check for the right place but would appreciate if you could pin-point me to a good place.

Closing this ticket.
I would guess in storage.conf man page? in github.com:containers/storage.
